summary:
Pit-and-fissure sealants have been shown to reduce carious lesions by up to 44%, making them highly effective against early decay in vulnerable tooth regions. They offer superior target-specific benefits compared to systemic fluoride alone and provide good long-term cost-effectiveness due to reduced need for dental restorations. 

(Note: This summary does not include non-relevant details such as parental involvement or broader public health policy.)

The provided information supports **pit-and-fissure sealants** being both clinically effective and economically sound among available child caries prevention methods because they significantly lower incidence rates at risk sites via precise placement and avoid costly treatments later. These findings align closely with scientific consensus regarding their role within comprehensive oral hygiene programs aimed primarily at preventing initial enamel breakdown caused by bacterial colonization in occlusal surfaces.
